title: Western Blot Protocol
author: Athan Dial
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rh3d38n

steps.instruction: Pull out comb gently and straight, avoid collapsing wells

Follow instructions provided with power pack to set up gels Short plate facing in on apparatus Press against gasket Press down and close arms simultaneously Put unit into bucket If not using 2 gels, use thick plast plate as the "dummy plate"
Fill inner chamber with 'fresh' 1x running buffer high enough to cover smaller plate and enter wells
Load protein ladder in right most lane *Note: gels are reversed, final product will have ladder on leftmost lane undetermined
Load samples with gel-laoding tips at consistent amount Tips: avoid bubbles and draw/eject samples slowly
Pour "used" 1x running buffer to fill bucket
Place the lid on the tank with matched colour-coded leads. Run the machine until protein ladder sufficiently separated. Undetermined, Room temperature 02:15:00, Constant 60 V
Make 1 L solution in "1x transfer buffer with MeOH" bottle undetermined, Distilled Water undetermined, Methanol undetermined, 10x Transfer Buffer
Put transfer buffer into -20 o C freezer #2 (bottom rack)
Fill wide plastic tray with 1x transfer buffer
Soak filter papers (6 in total per cassette) in 1x transfer buffer in tray
Carefully remove gel from between glass plates. Use the green plastic wedge.
Soak gels in transfer buffer for 5 minutes to equilibrate. Soak sponges as well.

Assemble immunoblot 'sandwich' in following order: Blot cassette with black (cathode) side down 2 sponges 3 sheets of soaked filter paper (roll out bubbles) Gel (with ladder on the right, dye front on the bottom) With tweezers, place PVDF membrane over gel 3 sheets of soaked filter paper (roll out bubbles) 2 sponges Close the cassette. Leave the cassette in transfer buffer until all cassettes are assembled
Using tweezers, obtain PVDF membrane and place in methanol on rocker. This activates the membrane.

Set up the power pack. Fill the bucket with the transfer buffer (should be about 3/4 full) Put in magnetic stir bar Put in ice pack into the bucket Ensure electrodes are in correct placement Start reaction in the cold room. Small bubbles should be seen when the reaction starts 01:30:00, Constant 90V
Block reagents and concentration will vary depending on antibodies. Usually 10 mL of block solution per plate.
Place membrane onto plate with tweezers. Add used amido black. Place plate on the rocker for 1 minute.
Cut the membrane with exacto-knife (if necessary). Take a picture.
Draw up excess amido black back into the bottle. Rinse the membrane with distilled water.
Place membrane on rocker with TBS-T to keep the membrane moist.
Remove previous TBS-T. Add block solution and incubate on rocker 01:00:00, Incubate on rocker
Defrost primary antibody solution 5 mins before completion of block incubation
Remove block solution and add primary antibody solution.
Incubate on a rocker within the cold room overnight . Keep the bottle with the membrane for reference purposes.
--- 14:00:00, Incubate with primary antibody (min) 18:00:00, Incubate with primary antibody (max)
Remove primary antibody with 10mL pipette. Keep the solution and put it back into its respective tube.
Wash membrane: Add 1x TBS-T to the membrane and incubate on the rocker 00:05:00, Wash/incubate on rocker
Perform wash step 3 times in total
Prepare secondary antibody solution. Diulent and % mass will vary depending on primary antibody used. 10 mL solution per plate. Do not contaminate antibodies.
Add secondary antibody to the membrane 01:00:00, Incubate on rocker
Wash membrane: Add 1x TBS-T to the membrane and incubate on the rocker 00:05:00, Wash/Incubate on rocker
Perform wash step 3 times in total
Keep membranes in TBS-T but remove from the rocker while preparing the ECL solution.
Set-up the imager. Turn on the computer Autofocus with the Mac card
Clean the square plate with EtOH. Ensure no particular matter
Prepare ECL solution in a black tube. Mix using the pipette. Use different pipette tips to prevent contamination. undetermined, Luminol undetermined, Clarity
Dap off excess TBS-T with KimWipe. Place membrane in square plate. Pipette out some of the ECL solution. In drop-wise fashion, just cover the membrane in the ECL solution.
Place container over the plate to avoid light 00:05:00, Incubate in solution
Using tweezers, put the membrane on the glass. Place the glass on the 3rd level in the machine in the middle of the tray.
Take preview image to see the ladder. Save the image.
Auto-expose and save the image
Soak membrane in 100% methanol and leave on a KimWipe to dry. Wrap the membrane in plastic wrap and label it. Undetermined, Store at RT
